topic: A Mongolian horseman using an Uurga to catch a wild horse, wide landscape shot.

parsed_generation.script:
Shot 1 [00:00-00:02]: Horseman readies the Uurga and studies the wild horse — front view across the steppe
Shot 2 [00:02-00:05]: Uurga loops around the wild horse, snaring it in the moment — close up
Shot 3 [00:05-00:08]: Horseman reins the captured horse, guiding it toward calm against the vast landscape — top view
parsed_generation.narration: Across a wide Mongolian landscape, a horseman uses an Uurga to catch a wild horse: he prepares, loops, and reins the animal into calm, in a concise three-shot sequence.

feedback: The sequence centers on a Mongolian horseman with an uurga capturing a wild horse, which roughly aligns with the topic. However, the gold standard emphasizes a wide establishing shot of the rider galloping with the uurga and a later shot of lowering the loop, whereas the generated sequence begins with readiness and ends with a top-view rein, lacking the dynamic gallop moment. To tighten alignment, restructure into two clear 4-second shots (wide establishing gallop, then extended loop to capture) and heighten the action-focused, cinematic language with explicit camera angles and dynamic motion.
strengths: Clear three-shot structure with explicit camera angles (front/steppe, close-up/uurga, top view).
Includes the uurga concept and the Mongolian steppe setting, keeping the subject centered.
fixes: Adopt the gold-standard two-shot timing: [00:00-00:04] wide establishing shot of rider galloping with the uurga, [00:04-00:08] close, action-driven sequence showing lowering the loop to capture the horse.

Use more vivid, action-first language: specify gallop speed, the loop extending toward the wild horse, and add a brief tactile cue (e.g., the wrist grip or the loop tightening) to convey motion and tension.
